Hi,

I'm using your module directly and indirectly via the SQLAlchemy-Utils module and its `URLType` column type which coerces a URL into a `furl` object.

I've found an issue when I try and use the `URLType` type on Python 3.x, I get the following error when I try and query an object using such a column:

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/query.py", line 2588, in all
    return list(self)
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/loading.py", line 86, in instances
    util.raise_from_cause(err)
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/util/compat.py", line 189, in raise_from_cause
    reraise(type(exception), exception, tb=exc_tb, cause=exc_value)
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/util/compat.py", line 183, in reraise
    raise value
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/loading.py", line 71, in instances
    rows = [proc(row) for row in fetch]
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/loading.py", line 71, in <listcomp>
    rows = [proc(row) for row in fetch]
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/loading.py", line 379, in _instance
    instance = session_identity_map.get(identitykey)
  File ".../venv-3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/identity.py", line 146, in get
    if key not in self._dict:
TypeError: unhashable type: 'furl'
```

I've managed to fix this by monkeypatching the `furl` class with a `__hash__` method like so:

```python
from sqlalchemy_utils import URLType
from furl import furl

def furl_hash(self):
    return hash(self.url)

furl.__hash__ = furl_hash

class Foo(Model):
    url = Column(URLType)
    ...
```

Would it be possible to add such a method? I'm not sure if I'm 100% correct just using the value of `self.url` here but it looked close enough.

vint poorly handle functions and their scopes
issue_body:
Hello.

Consider the following VimL code:
```
call vundle#begin('~/.vim/plugins/')
Plugin 'todesking/vint-syntastic'
Plugin 'scrooloose/syntastic'
call vundle#end()
```

vint complains:
```
Make the scope explicit like `g:vundle#begin` (see Anti-pattern of vimrc (Scope of variable))
Make the scope explicit like `g:vundle#end` (see Anti-pattern of vimrc (Scope of variable))
```
The comment about `Scope of variable` is out of place here. Anti-pattern of Vim also says nothing about function scopes.

Now consider the following code that describes a function that should be available globally:
```
function! mysuperplugin#do()
    echom 'Hello world!'
endfunction
```
Again vint complains:
```
Make the scope explicit like `g:mysuperplugin#do` (see Anti-pattern of vimrc (Scope of variable))
```

Logically this function should have `g:` scope, but Vim actually forbids it:
```
The name must be made of alphanumeric characters and '_',
and must start with a capital or "s:" (see above).
Note that using "b:" or "g:" is not allowed.
```
See `:h E124` for more info.

While the previous behavior is still somewhat tolerable, the following code:
```
function! g:mysuperplugin#do()
    echom 'Hello world!'
endfunction
```
is valid for vint, while for Vim it is clearly not. This is really bad.

Please fix.

Any of the following would be better than the current state of affairs:

- joke2k.net faker docs don't exist or merely redirect to extended docs at RTD
- RTD doesn't exist, and joke2k.net has the actual extended docs
- joke2k.net just mirrors the README off the repo and clearly links to the extended docs on RTD (worst option IMHO, better to just have a README on the repo, and one set of offsite extended docs)

As part of this, I'd suggest changing the URL at the top of the repo to point to the extended docs, whether that ends up being on RTD or on joke2k.net

issue_title: Doing work in a plugin __init_() hangs startup
issue_body:
With this code:
````
class H5Handoff(WillPlugin):
    def __init__(self):
        self.h5s_heard = []
         r = redis.Redis()
        try:
            res = json.loads(r.get("current_sre_oncall"))
            self.current = res
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning("failed to fetch oncall: %s", e)
            return None
````

Will never starts up, or even begins writing to redis for the scheduler lock. It hangs after print this to the console:
````
Starting core processes
  ✓ Web server started.
  ✓ Scheduler started.
````
Removing the redis call makes it startup cleanly again. Moving this code into a different method and invoking it after startup via @hear works fine. I'm not sure if it's because I'm talking to redis, doing I/O in general, or what.
for now. (or nothing shows up with a bunch of error messages).

issue_title: reversed tickets.audits generator behavior seems inconsistent
issue_body:
Running this code after creating client `zpc`:

```
revgen=reversed(zpc.tickets.audits(limit=5))
for i,audit in enumerate(revgen):
    if i%5==0:
        print()
        if i==15:
            break
    print(audit.created.time())
```

Gives this output:

```
02:19:39
03:01:08
03:01:09
03:01:10
04:01:44

02:18:24
02:03:07
02:01:56
02:01:55
02:01:55

02:01:55
02:01:55
02:01:21
01:43:44
01:41:48
```

The first chunk of results are in chronologically increasing order, whereas each subsequent chunk of results are in decreasing order (as is expected if one is running backwards in time).

Saving the user the effort of chaining together a series of chunks, by implicitly retrieving and passing on the before_cursor, is appealing, but it's not entirely clear that `reversed` is a clear / intuitive way of doing this, given that the default order of the returned audits generator is already backwards in time, and leading to counter-intuitive behavior such as reported here.

xref https://github.com/facetoe/zenpy/issues/191#issuecomment-363300838

issue_title: Training and running dnns on CPU with MKL Multithreaded is slower than with MKL Sequential
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
Training and running a dnn on CPU with dlib linked against the multithreaded version of MKL should give a speedup of (CPU_Cores - 1) compared to MKL sequential.

## Current Behavior
Training and running a dnn on CPU with dlib linked against the multithreaded version of MKL results in a performance loss of about 10% compared to MKL sequential. Additionally cpu time is wasted in the multithreaded case since all cores run on 100% instaed of one core beeing used in sequential mode.

## Steps to Reproduce
1.: Build the examples of dlib without cuda
2.: Train a dnn example (I used the mnist example dnn_introduction_ex).
3.: Time the execution of the dnn like in  https://github.com/Zyrin/dlib/commit/13caad723e8922e660e54dc2d871d22dada98535
4.: Rebuild the example with PullRequest https://github.com/davisking/dlib/pull/1302 and cmake option DLIB_USE_MKL_SEQUENTIAL enabled
5.: Rerun the same timing programm

* **Version**: 19.8 + 19.10
* **Where did you get dlib**: dlib.net
* **Platform**: Windows 7 + 10 64 bit and Ubuntu 16.04.4 LTS 64 bit
* **Compiler**: Visual Studio 2015 + gcc  5.4

issue_title: using a config file from the current directory is insecure
issue_body:
Especially since the config file allows arbitrary code to be loaded, using one from whatever directory the user happens to be in when the keyring module runs is insecure.  
There doesn't seem to be anything to prevent an unscrupulous person from creating a config file that loads a module that reveals the authentication information.
The config file from the user's home directory should be loaded first, and the current directory's config file loaded only if explicitly enabled in the home directory config file.
Also, the config files should not be used if they are owned by anyone other that the current user, or if they are writable by anyone else.

issue_title: Screen shots are blank under MacOS
issue_body:
It seems the MacOS implementation of the window doesn't render with visible=false. This affects both the following ways of generating an image:

`mesh.scene().save_image('image.png')`
and
`mesh.show(save_image='image.png')`

As a work around, could you please add the option to set the `visible` flag to `True` even when using `save_image`?

Otherwise the workaround is to duplicate much of SceneViewer() to force `visible` to `True`

issue_title: Unable to create record for delegated subdomain
issue_body:
I am able to use Lexicon to create records on our top level domain (`platformservices.io`) which is hosted by Gandi, but not our delegated subdomains, hosted in Route53.

I get error `StandardError: No domain found`

Looking at the Route53 provider, it loops through Route53 zones it has found to see where your domain fits:

https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/master/lexicon/providers/route53.py#L94

But the way the domain name is being parsed means it never gets matched.  I added a print line to show the domain it was trying to match to and it showed` platformservices.io.`

The zone in Route53, however is `euw.platformservices.io`, so the `euw` is being stripped in order to find the zone name.

Do you have any plan to extended rdpy to include clipboard support?  Any idea where one should start to look if they wanted to take a stab at implementing this feature?

Thanks!

Hi,

I love vcrpy!
now that tornado support is included, any chance of supporting aiohttp anytime soon?

Cheers
These tests need improved

issue_title: Memory leak in adodbapi (resources not released when cursor goes out of scope)
issue_body:
I've been tearing my hair out trying to find out why some of our long-running jobs were exhausting all of the memory on the servers. I finally tracked it down to a failure of the `adodbapi` `Cursor` objects to release the memory of the resources under their control when they go out of scope. The following code snippet will eventually exhaust all memory on the machine when running under `adodbapi`:

```python
while True:
    cursor = conn.cursor()
```
With other DB API 2.0 packages (for example, `pyodbc`), memory usage remains flat running this same code, as all of their resources are released when the cursor objects goes out of scope.

A workaround is to explicitly close all cursor objects before they go out of scope everywhere in your code.

issue_title: Serializing properties which may or may not be present in object being passed in
issue_body:
Forgive me if my terminology is a bit off.  So far I've been pretty impressed with Serpy - we were able to get some serious performance out of it compared to the Django Rest Framework serializer when returning several thousand objects in a single call (don't ask).

I have run into a small challenge, and I'm not quite sure the best way to overcome it.  I've got a Serializer that inherits from serpy.Serializer, which has a number of fields (let's call them a, b, c, d, etc.).  These fields may or may not be present in the object which I need to serialize, but I'd always like to make sure that they are present with a value of None in the serialized data.  So my serializer looks something like:
```
class MySerializer(serpy.Serializer):
    a = serpy.FloatField()
    b = serpy.FloatField()
    c = serpy.FloatField()
    # ... and so on, for a bunch of fields
```
Inside of a Django view, the QuerySet of objects is filtered, and passed into the serializer.
```
# someObjects is a QuerySet of objects, which may or may not contain a, b, c, etc.
serializer = MySerializer(someObjects, many=True)
```
This raises the following exception:
```
... views.py", line 80, in get
    return Response(serializer.data)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\serpy\serializer.py", line 139, in data
    self._data = self.to_value(self.instance)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\serpy\serializer.py", line 128, in to_value
    return [serialize(o, fields) for o in instance]
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\serpy\serializer.py", line 116, in _serialize
    result = to_value(result)
TypeError: float() argument must be a string or a number```
Switching the FloatFields to be required=False no longer raises the exception, but it no longer puts the corresponding dictionary key in the output.  Ideally I would like the value of a, b, c, etc. to always be present in the output but with a value of None.  Is there a way to do this en-mass, without having to do something for every field I have that is like this?

Several of the fully unit-tested libraries have _very_ large XML files for certain modules. Much of this is duplicated settings that could equally well apply to many of the tests in the module. For example `eps` as a finite precision tolerance is ubiquitous in numeric codes; `verbose` or `seed` for random numbers will also be common to many of the tests. This is an enhancement specification for simplifying the XML needed to generate the tests. In many cases, we could reduce the tests to about 40% the number of lines.

## Global Variable Assignments

Have a new tag `<global>` in the `<fortpy>` namespace of the XML file. It can have two children:

### `<value>` Tag

This has the same attributes as the `<value>` tag that is child of `<assignment>`. However, it can be applied to _any_ assignment in the unit tests of the same file. Any assignment would reference it with:

```xml
<assignment name="variable" value="global:id" />
```

where the `id` of the `<value>` tag is unique in the `<global>` list. We would pass in a global dictionary of the XML elements in the `<global>` tag. Each time such a `<value>` is encountered, we would just add its reference to the parent `<assignment>` tags values list.

### `<defaults>` Tag

Allows default attributes to be set that are common to _any_ other tag in the same file that usually mandates that attribute, but doesn't have it set. For example, the `<output>` tag requires a `folder` attribute to be set so that we know where the file lives. Instead, the `folder` attribute would be omitted and a `<defaults>` tag would be included in `<global>` as follows:

```xml
<defaults folder="../tests/utilities" staging="../unittests/" />
```

Before the `ValueError` is thrown on any tag in the file during XML parsing, `fortpy` would first check the global `<defaults>` for an attribute with that value. The downside to such automation is that a person may forget why something isn't working only to discover that we were using the default values because they didn't specify the attribute at a lower level. I think the shorter files are worth the tradeoff; they could always just not use `<global>` if they are worried.

## Automated Identifiers

There is a one-to-one correspondence between `<target>` and `<output>` tags (usually) and for most tests, only one parameter or variable is having its value checked. In such an instance, we should not require the `<output>` tag to have its identifier set and then `compareto` attribute on the `<target>` tag could be left out as well. We could just hook them up since there is only one way to do it.

Most variable assignments have an `<assignment>` tag with a single `<value>` embedded in it. If there is only one value, the user should be allowed to leave out `identifier` on the `<value>` tag and the attribute `value` on the `<assignment>` tag since there is only one way to hook those up. If no value should be assigned, and the `<value>` tag is being kept for backup or working then an identifier on `<value>` would _prevent_ the auto-assignment of the `value` attribute.

For most unit tests, there is only one `<test>` tag. We should auto-assign the identifier of the test tag in this case to be `default`, set `timed="true"` by default and set a default description such as "No additional descriptors.". Thus, only `cases` would need to be explicitly set if the test is run for multiple cases.

For the `<group>` tag that defines the unit tests, it should be sufficient to have `purpose="testing"` on the tag. We could use the name of the `executable_test` as the default identifier for the group.

## `<auto>` Tag

Many of the unit tests have massive duplication of lots of attributes. For example, most tests store the input and output files in the same directory relative to the testing root. Most of the filenames have the same form (e.g. `sometest_varname.in.{}`). The `<auto>` tag allows such duplication to be swept under the guise of `$` as in this example:

```xml
	<auto names="js.out, ebars.out, rms.out, err.out">
	  <output file="bcs_reweight_$.logsum.{}" />
	</auto>
	<auto names="eps, seed"><assignment value="global:$" /></auto>
```

The first `<auto>` tag would create _four_ `<output>` tags that are all identical except that 1) for `file` the `$` would be replaced by the name in the comma-separated list; 2) `identifier` on each `<output>` would be set to the name from the comma-separated list.

The second `<auto>` tag would create variable assignments for both `eps` and `seed` and set their values to the `<global>` `<value>` tags with the identifiers of their names. 

For the `<auto>` tags, we would perform a deep copy of the XML tag and then update only the relevant attributes for each kind of tag (either `name` or `identifier` depending on its purpose), and then replacing the `$` in each attribute in the child tag.

Overall, this will make the tests much quicker to write and easier to read. It shouldn't take too long either.

That is what I present to pyrebase as data format, called `dbdata`. Be aware that the whole data structure is presented to the Pyrebase wrapper as a Python dict, where `dictofattributes` is a nested dict.

What I do to push the data into Pyrebase is:
```
db.child("id").push(dbdata, user['idToken'])
```
If I try the nested dict, I get the following error:
```
Invalid data; couldn't parse JSON object, array, or value.
```

I have tried several things including converting the nested dict into a json file and then reconverting it into a dict before pushing, assuming it is a format problem. 

Those solutions haven't worked. However if I pass the nested dict as a list of nested lists instead, it passes without conflict.

**EDIT**: forgot to mention that I am generating the naming and values of the nested dict dynamically. It seems not to be a problem if I explicitly assign a hard-coded dict to `dictofattributes`.

imaplib does not by default include a timeout on the request. This can cause the request (and thus the program) to hang when calling the server, line [190](https://github.com/JimmXinu/FanFicFare/blob/master/fanficfare/geturls.py#L190), since it never gives up on the connection and tries again (or just abandons completely).

I'm not sure how you handle timeouts in anything else (for the writers and such) but the simplest way is

`import socket
socket.setdefaulttimeout(30)`

at some point before the line is called.

According to issue #107 already solved, I can restart my BACnet server without restarting my whole application.
For another purpose, I activated the bacpypes internal debug logs and I discovered a very strange log:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/calligp/workspace/P5.4/POC/bacpypes/py27/bacpypes/core.py", line 151, in run
    asyncore.loop(timeout=delta, count=1)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/asyncore.py", line 220, in loop
    poll_fun(timeout, map)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/asyncore.py", line 145, in poll
    r, w, e = select.select(r, w, e, timeout)
error: (9, 'Bad file descriptor')
```

This error appears after a warm restart and the file concerned is the broadcast socket from `UDPMultiplexer`.

Symptoms are particularly difficult to understand:
* When client initialize request, the operation is processed and the response is received.
* When server sends a notification (for example COV notification), the client receives nothing.

After many research about socket creation and deletion, I finnaly found the cause of this issue.
It is the class `UDPDirectory` that is derived from `asyncore.dispatcher` which doesn't end communication on the broadcast socket.
This dispatcher must be properly closed using close() function.

See attached patch.

[0007_RestartSocket.txt](https://github.com/JoelBender/bacpypes/files/1369086/0007_RestartSocket.txt)

Maybe same correction can be applied on TCP communication process but I can not test it.

Moreover, I found another minor issue in UDPMultiplexer class regarding broadcasePort:
When the broadcast port is not required, the attribut self.broadcastPort is not initialized (not even to None value).
Then I suggest you to initialize it. It is present in the same patch.

We only have doctests right now :(.

I think starting with the Python converter is probably the easiest place to start. Then we can write tests for the JS.

issue_title: Tests using strategies with invalid arguments should error at execution time, not definition
issue_body:
See discussion on #22 for details.

The problem in this case is that @mulkieran wants to be able to skip tests in cases where they have an invalid strategy involved (e.g. because of an empty sampled from), but in general it seems a bit rude for a user error to stop executing tests entirely.

The solution is to raise the InvalidArgument error at test execution time instead of definition.

I propose the following solution:

1. Create an 'InvalidStrategy' SearchStrategy subtype that can not generate examples but will raise InvalidArgument on *any* of the standard strategy methods called on it. It will do this by first calling a custom function which is expected to raise InvalidArgument and asserting false afterwards to prevent fallthrough.
2. Create a catches_invalid decorator for use in hypothesis.strategies. This executes the called function and returns its result, but if an InvalidArgument is thrown creates instead an InvalidStrategy which will re-execute the relevant function at test time.

This should mean you get exactly the same InvalidArgument exceptions, but they happen within the tests instead of at the definition time. In particular it becomes possible to skip tests that would otherwise raise InvalidArgument.

@mulkieran, can you confirm whether you think this would satisfy your use case?

Quickcheck lets you label examples and then at the end reports a breakdown of the statistics for different labels.

I’ve never got around to implementing this in Hypothesis despite the fact that I’ve been intending to do so since even before 1.0. Part of why is that it matters less for Hypothesis – one of the classic reasons you want this is because Quickcheck can easily generate a lot of duplicate examples if you’re not careful. Hypothesis generally doesn’t do that because of its built in deduplication.

Still, it would be a useful feature which I would like to implement at some point.

issue_title: Problems with type aliases inside template
issue_body:
Hi!

I am trying to use Breathe to document a template class.  I am using `breathe` master, since I am aware that other releases have problems (as of #213).  The relevant part of the template class looks like this:
```cpp
template <typename T,
          detail::rbts::bits_t B = default_bits,
          typename MemoryPolicy  = default_memory_policy>
class flex_vector
{
public:
    using value_type = T;
    using reference = const T&;
    using size_type = std::size_t;
    using difference_type = std::ptrdiff_t;
    using const_reference = const T&;
    ...
```

Nothing crazy I believe, just the typical type aliases when defining a container. However, I am having problems with Breathe. On the command line, it reports stuff like:
```
immer/doc/index.md:1: WARNING: Too many template argument lists compared to parameter lists. Argument lists: 1, Parameter lists: 0, Extra empty parameters lists prepended: 1. Declaration:
	immer::flex_vector<T, B, MemoryPolicy>::value_type
...
```

And the generated documentation looks like this:
![screenshot from 2016-10-27 20-59-47](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/4521138/19781172/6e4418c8-9c88-11e6-81a7-d77dc00ef460.png)

I have these two comments:

- The `using` keyword should only appear once. I guess that the `using >` thingy was not intended to appear and this is a bug.

- The `immer::flex_vector<T, B, MemoryPolicy>::` part in front of every type is verbose and redundant. Is there any way to make it not appear? That might be what is causing the problem, but I have not found a way to make `doxygen` not generate it as part of the definition. Maybe `breathe` itself could remove it otherwise, what do you think? If you guide me, I can prepare a patch for this.

Thanks!

---

issue_title: Work in Unicode for byte patterns and Unicode patterns
issue_body:
One thing that backrefs does that is inefficient is slicing over and over as we walk the string.  This is because bytes don't work the same across all Python versions, and in certain cases indexing a position gives you the value, not the character.

It seems that both Regex and Re take byte strings and decode them to Latin-1.  This would make things much easier to navigate.  And while we are rebuilding the pattern we could just covert the pieces back to byte string.

I'd like to mimic this behavior to clean up the code and improve performance.

issue_title: Issue in finding a solid-colour image
issue_body:
I have encountered a trouble in finding a mono-colour image.  

1. Set the desktop background to a solid colour.  
2. Take a screenshot1 of the blank area of the desktop, e.g. 1500x900 pixels, without any icon.  The file is a solid-colour image and its size is about 5k.
3. Take another screenshot2 including left side shortcut icons, e.g. 1900x900 pixels.  The file size is about 90k.
4. Open a program on the desktop, e.g. notepad 1000x800 pixels, and position it in the 1500x900 area.
5. Run find(screenshot1.png) and find(screenshot2.png).

The expected result is that both 'find' will fail, because there is a significant notepad dialog in the region blocking the background.

The actual outcome was that only the second 'find' failed.  The first 'find' was successful and it reported:
Found match for pattern 'C:\temp\testPNG.sikuli\monoBG.png' at (0,0) with confidence (1.0). Target at (750, 450)

Tested using Sikuli, it was able to fail the find(screenshot1).

Issue was found in 0.4.0a1.  (It has been working well for me so I haven't upgraded it yet.)

Thanks.

issue_title: Can JWTs be assigned without going to /auth?
issue_body:
I've switched over all of my auth mechanisms to use JWTs across the board, but there is one workflow that would be great if there was an easy way to spit out a JWT from the library. 

In my user registration workflow, they fill in their data, I add them to the database (after validation), I send them a confirmation mail, but now I'm requiring them to re-login to go through the @jwt_required workflow so I can return a JWT. 

After registration, I want them to log in directly, so is there a way to extract a JWT (given that they just gave me their username/pass), without having to re-encode them manually?

Thanks!
-SJ

issue_title: JSONField default passed to SQL create column query
issue_body:
Summary: Migrations created to add a JSONField include default, causing query to fail.

Django Version: Django 1.9.2-8

Database and version used: MySQL 5.7.11

Version: Django-MySQL 1.0.1

Hi,
I've been having some inconsistent behaviour with this so sorry if this is confusing. In the current situation I'm stuck in, I have model that was created with a JSONField without any problems, and I'm trying to add another JSONField. It keeps failing because the migrations tries to set a default on the field, which MySQL doesn't allow:
(`django.db.utils.OperationalError: (1101, "BLOB, TEXT, GEOMETRY or JSON column 'tables' can't have a default value")`)
I think I know why this is happening: https://github.com/django/django/blob/6bf7964023487f2a352084e74aca27aecb354d6c/django/db/backends/mysql/schema.py#L28
That method tells migrations to leave out the default for certain fields, and I believe JSONField would now need to be included among those, as the django_mysql JSONField implicitly sets `dict` as its default.

I can't immediately see how this could be rectified here. Do you think it requires a bug report for Django? Or maybe I've overlooked something.

I know I've left out full tracebacks and stuff but that's just because I'm pretty sure I've located the issue above. Let me know if you'd like more.

Some of the boto services (i.e. RDS) don't have a ``region``-setting ``~/.boto`` option, and the only way to set the connection region for them is in whatever is using boto (i.e. this project). We need to support setting regions.

For the time being, we're not adding multi-region support to awslimitchecker, we're just adding support to set regions other than boto's hard-coded ``us-east-1`` default. We'll need to pass this to the individual service connectors, TA, and anything else that connects to AWS.

Possible options:
1. Make this a command-line option, and also have a way to set it on the main Checker class from Python.
2. In all of our service connectors, simply check for an ``AWS_REGION`` environment variable, and pass it in to the connector if it's specified.

issue_title: Adding support to marshallers to require modules not present and read back approximate versions of the data, as well as lazy loading of required modules
issue_body:
Adding marshallers to support types in more modules of the main Python libraries as well as external packages would increase loading time due to loading more packages and add dependencies for the way marshallers work in the 0.1.x series.

Thus, the parent marshaller class, ``hdf5storage.Marshallers.TypeMarshaller`` is being changed to support lists of required modules that can be lazy loaded when the marshaller is actually needed, and if they are not available, a ``read_approximate`` method to read back an approximate version of the data using standard python types and numpy types to be able to at least read something. The ``MarshallerCollection`` class as well as other classes are being changed to support this feature.

This is an API change, but it doesn't break compatibility so long as ``hdf5storage.Marshallers.TypeMarshaller`` or its descendants are still inherited from. Completely custom marshallers will no longer be compatible.

issue_title: When user_load return None, session['user_id'] should be cleared
issue_body:
My custom `user_loader` queries the database for an user. Between requests, the user can be invalidated in the database (either when the column `inactive_after` has been reached, or when this column is changed). In this case, my `user_loader` returns `None`. 

After this point, the user is logged out. `flask_login.current_user.is_authenticated` is `False` and everything seems fine.

However, with every request, the user_loader is called with the old `user_id` which bears no meaning anymore. Also, at this point there is no need for flask_login to call `user_loader`.

I fixed this at this moment by setting the user_id session to `None`:

    @login_manager.user_loader
    def user_loader(id):
        user = SessionUser.find_by_session_id(id) #hits the database
        if user==None:
            flash('You have been logged out')
            session['user_id'] = None
        return user

However, I think this should be solved within flash_login itself. I think [reload_user](https://github.com/maxcountryman/flask-login/blob/41686db687a94a7a2eb2aa4b0abc3007b02bd54a/flask_login/login_manager.py#L305) should be changed, and have added the line `session['user_id'] = None`.

I'm happy to create a PR. Let me know what you think! I'm new to flask and I'm here to learn.

issue_title: README example does not emit records
issue_body:
README provides quick example of usage:

    import logging

    import json_log_formatter

    formatter = json_log_formatter.JSONFormatter()

    json_handler = logging.FileHandler(filename='/var/log/my-log.json')
    json_handler.setFormatter(formatter)

    logger = logging.getLogger('my_json')
    logger.addHandler(json_handler)

    logger.info('Sign up', extra={'referral_code': '52d6ce'})

Testing that on Debian Jessie 64-bit with Python 2.7.9 and using `JSON-log-formatter==0.0.2` there
is no output in the log file.

The reason is simple, default log level is WARN and example creates INFO log entries.

There are two options, either set log level to INFO: `logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)`  or to create log records by
    `logger.warn('Sign up', extra={'referral_code': '52d6ce'})`

issue_title: Add a hard line break extension
issue_body:
I not a big fan of the double space notation for line breaks when not using the `nl2br` extension.  I can never see those double spaces. I have seen some parsers like, I think, Pandoc and CommonMark use `\` to escape the newline to signify a line break.  That is cool, but that still bothers me a little.  If I want to do:

c:\some\windows\path\

It would require me to escape the `\`.  I don't want to have to escape it like this `c:\some\windows\path\\`.  It's just another thing I don't want to think about.

So instead I would like something like `Some text \`.  It's basically the same, but with a space in front.  I can probably make the Pandoc/CommonMark way an option, but out the box I want it to not be annoying and drop in without really messing with the majority of docs.  I probably wouldn't make it officially loaded via the `pymdown` extension until I had a new major release.

Anyways, this would be an extremely easy extension to throw together.

I am still on the fence about this.  I've thrown together the extension, I just need to decide if I want to pull the trigger.

issue_title: Arithmatex: Script insert can have previews -- move to script insert
issue_body:
MathJax will actually hide `MathJax_Preview` classes, so script tag is a viable option for conversion content if you also want a preview. This makes conversion of `\[\]` or `\(\)` obsolete.  The only reason script was not done by default was the lack of a preview, but preview is apparently possible with script.

Wrap options can be deprecated (`tex_inline_wrap` and `tex_block_wrap`) and so can `insert_as_script`.  Instead, we can always use script and allow the user to disable the preview if they like.

Dear all,
The import_from_csv() code contains the [following lines](https://github.com/web2py/pydal/blob/master/pydal/objects.py#L989) :

```python
   # create new id until we get the same as old_id+offset
   while curr_id < csv_id+id_offset[self._tablename]:
         self._db(self._db[self][colnames[cid]] == curr_id).delete()
```

Inserted records can thus be removed several times before they reach their final `id`. If there is in the record any `upload` field, this will trigger `delete_uploaded_files()`, removing files when `autodelete` is set.

I find that names like HybridBlock, HybridSequential, and hybridize() in gluon are not effectively conveying what their utilities and usages are. To me, 'HybridBlock' sounds like a mixture of 'Block' and something else, although it is actually an extension of a Block. Also, calling hybridize() sounds like imperative and symbolic program will be mixed in executions, although in reality, calling it makes an one-way transition of the block from an imperative program to a symbolic program. Lastly, 'hybrid' is an ambiguous word which meaning is unclear until a user takes a careful look at the hybrid computation tutorial.

Proposal: Why don't we call these CompilableBlock, CompilableSequential, and compile()? Then, it should be clear CompilableBlocks are extensions of Blocks, and executions of CompilableBlocks will be faster after calling compile() than they were before, because 'compile' is a concept most programmers are familiar with.

Furthermore, from the API design point of view, it might be clearer if compile() creates a new Block rather than changing the state of an exiting HybridBlock. Then it should be clear that CompilableBlock is still an imperative program, whereas CompilableBlock.compile() is a declarative program.

I am curious what others' thoughts on this are. Tagging @szha since I talked with him on this separately.

issue_title: About the quality of this repository
issue_body:
Hi dear maintainers,

I've been pretty active on this repository for a long time now, and I must say that I'm pretty pissed with the quality of the code. I know that _THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND_ but, still, you cannot just put your official python SDK out there and say "oh it's fine if its broken, just use PHP instead".

Where I'm working, we've been using the Facebook Ads API for several years and went through all the different migration adventures, not without breaking a sweat. I must say that when I saw the [changelog](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/apps/changelog) for the 2.6 version, I told myself "Piece of cake". Yeah, right. I've opened 4 issues on this repo so far:
- https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/pull/222
- https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/pull/223
- https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/issues/224
- https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/issues/221

And the deadline to migrate on this version is set 5 days from now, July 13th.

But that's not all. You introduced an auto-generation of code. I don't know who had this idea but it's pretty far from genius. So I get it, you don't want to have to maintain 250+ python classes manually. But hey, have you thought about inheritance? Have you thought about meta classes? Instead here is what we get:
- obfuscated code: I didn't know it could exist in Python, but you made it possible
- almost impossible collaboration: a nice guy wants to improve your data model? he can't
- poorly designed python code: yes, python is supposed to be a scripting language. But do you think the Django guys are writing scripts?
- non-standard code: have you tried to run `pep8` or `pylint` on your codebase?

Oh and that's still not all. There is simply no unit tests. The only ones that are here were coded by collaborators not working at Facebook. Today, you have:
- 32 unit tests
- 7 integration tests (BTW, do you even run them?)
- 250+ python files

Don't you think that instead of auto-generating python code you could have written unit tests?

Last but not least, today you have 30 opened issues/PRs, and some have been opened for a long time. Can you improve your processes regarding that matter? I think it's sad that people are spending time on this SDK and you just ignore their work.

Of course I'm gonna continue to use this repo because I have no other choice, but I and other developers would be very glad if you could work on those improvements.

Have a good day,
Max.

http://api.jquery.com/category/deprecated/deprecated-1.8/

- [x] Docs in warnings.md for `.andSelf()` and `.size()` (Ref #62)
- [x] `.load()`
- [x] `.unload()`
- [x] `deferred.pipe()`

N.B. Since the `.load()` and `.unload()` shims are just like [`.error()`](https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/683b9b3ae40e3fe9e83f3dd39d8e5e958975f3fa/src/event.js#L41-51) the existing code can just be generalized. None of those need to bubble so `.triggerHandler()` will do the trick.

issue_title: Graph for exporting handles sequence lengths incorrectly
issue_body: The current code just uses the shape of the input tensor to calculate sequence lengths, but that only works if all the examples in the batch have the same length. The sequence lengths need to be an additional input for that graph to work in all cases.

issue_title: There's no way to restore a checkpoint and do just testing
issue_body: If you try to set ds_epochs to exactly last_checkpoint+1 and set ds_restore_checkpoint to 1 something in the code that measures how long each epoch takes breaks. So you have to set ds_epochs to last_checkpoint+2 and do an epoch of training.

issue_body:
Hi all, just a quick question. 

run-cluster.sh + DeepSpeech.py seem to only cover data-parallelism implementation and model parallelism implementation described in the paper does not exist as of now. Do you have plans for developing this feature? 

I was about to modify the code and develop this feature on my own but wanted to check if the feature already exists or is being developed before moving on.

issue_title: Unexpected behavior for language maps for data w/o language
issue_body:
Behavior Encountered:

When compacting a context with a language map, when the data does not have languages assigned, the key specified in the context is not used.  When the context specifies a default language, this is also not used when the input format is nquads, but IS used when the input is JSON-LD.  This should at least be consistent, and preferably the default language assigned.

Behavior Expected:

When compacting data, the default language specified in the context should be used.

Specification Reference:
  https://www.w3.org/TR/json-ld/#string-internationalization

Playground Example:
    http://tinyurl.com/hr7rwl8

Test Case:
  [test-langmap.zip](https://github.com/digitalbazaar/pyld/files/480742/test-langmap.zip)

Related Issues:
  https://github.com/digitalbazaar/jsonld.js/issues/151

issue_title: add 3840x2160 fanart as accepted in the check
Filemodification times contain no timezone information
issue_body:
The attributes `created_on` and `modified_on` of `sevenbridges.models.file.File` don't have associated timezone information (and `f.modified_on.tzinfo is None` is `True`), and they are not UTC timestamps either.

Specifically, given a file `f` on the platform, the following is not true:

```python
f.modified_on == datetime.fromtimestamp(timegm(f.modified_on.timetuple()))
```

In my case the difference is exactly one hour. Ideally these timestamps would always be UTC, but if they are not, they should at least have timezone information.

issue_title: Replace the scenario_damage calculator with the oq-lite one
issue_body: This requires a lot of preliminary work. First the oq-lite calculator needs to be enhanced to be able to read  GMFs in NRML format, so that the feature --load-gmf is not lost. Then the oq-lite calculator must be able to store its outputs in the datastore, so that they can be exported from the engine.

issue_title: Importing the GMFs from an XML file is broken

As discovered by Parisa on the mailing list https://groups.google.com/d/msg/openquake-users/oxbWg7lRgbI/xrxa9FhYCAAJ

issue_title: Setting Content-Length still results in Transfer-Encoding set to "chunked"
issue_body:
I'm encountering an issue where uploading a file and setting the ContentLength header in the `put_object()` call still results in the Transfer-Encoding being set to chunked. This, in turn, results in the failure to upload a file, with the following error: 2016-05-05 23:37:25,374 botocore.vendored.requests.packages.urllib3.connectionpool [DEBUG] "PUT /AUTH_test/test/bash_logout HTTP/1.1" 501 None DEBUG:botocore.vendored.requests.packages.urllib3.connectionpool:"PUT /AUTH_test/test/bash_logout HTTP/1.1" 501 None 2016-05-05 23:37:25,388 botocore.parsers [DEBUG] Response headers: {'x-amz-id-2': 'ZTpbRioCBOJR3RwdOUDSHgwMBABo8O8oFSpmvyG7F3turQ6Q9MPRg5JMjxI49Rk57MXudMm6lSQ=', 'server': 'AmazonS3', 'transfer-encoding': 'chunked', 'connection': 'close', 'x-amz-request-id': '008D7F33A19459E1', 'date': 'Thu, 05 May 2016 23:37:17 GMT', 'content-type': 'application/xml'} DEBUG:botocore.parsers:Response headers: {'x-amz-id-2': 'ZTpbRioCBOJR3RwdOUDSHgwMBABo8O8oFSpmvyG7F3turQ6Q9MPRg5JMjxI49Rk57MXudMm6lSQ=', 'server': 'AmazonS3', 'transfer-encoding': 'chunked', 'connection': 'close', 'x-amz-request-id': '008D7F33A19459E1', 'date': 'Thu, 05 May 2016 23:37:17 GMT', 'content-type': 'application/xml'} 2016-05-05 23:37:25,397 botocore.parsers [DEBUG] Response body: NotImplementedA header you provided implies functionality that is not implemented
Transfer-Encoding
008D7F33A19459E1ZTpbRioCBOJR3RwdOUDSHgwMBABo8O8oFSpmvyG7F3turQ6Q9MPRg5JMjxI49Rk57MXudMm6lSQ= I believe the issue is in the `botocore/vendored/requests/models.py` file, specifically: ``` 440 if is_stream: 441 body = data 442 443 if files: 444 raise NotImplementedError('Streamed bodies and files are mutually exclusive.') 445 446 if length is not None: 447 self.headers['Content-Length'] = builtin_str(length) 448 else: 449 self.headers['Transfer-Encoding'] = 'chunked' ``` My invocation is as follows: ``` s3_client.put_object(Bucket=self.aws_bucket, Key=key, Body=input_stream, ContentLength=length) ``` and I expect it to set the ContentLength and not use the chunked encoding. I'll submit a patch to consider the contents of the headers when determining which encoding to use (which also unblocks my usage of boto3).

issue_title: Traceback on dependency resolution error with editable git
issue_body:
When I have an editable git repository and another requirement that causes a dependency resolution failure in my Pipfile, I get a `FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '"/usr/bin/python3"': '"/usr/bin/python3"'` error.

My reproducer uses https://github.com/OddBloke/sphinx-git and python-novaclient==2.26.0 (which always causes a dependency resolution error, as mentioned in #1232).

(As far as I can tell, the git repository and the other requirement don't need to be at all related to end up with this traceback.)

##### Describe your environment

The current logic behind FluentRecordFormatter is to only log the attributes that are specified with keys and values being formatted. This is too limiting as [LoggerAdapter](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logging.LoggerAdapter) and extra fields allow the same formatter to be used with different sets of extra fields, all of which need to get transmitted to FluentD. Furthermore this logic requires to create a handler-formatter pair and add each one to distinct logger if different data set types are send from different loggers.

There are [standard fields](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logrecord-attributes) that will *always* occur on the [LogRecord](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logging.LogRecord). All other firelds would be custom user-specified ones, including extra.

The solution, to my mind, is simple: provide a mode, where instead of inclusion dictionary and exclusion dictionary is used that will filter out the fields that we don't want to send through formatter. If a exclusion dictionary is `None`, we assume current behavior. If exclusion dictionary is `set()/[]/()` we send everything. In all other cases we exclude the keys from the specified set.

related to #47

issue_title: io.imshow_collection always creating a new figure
issue_body:
I am using `io.imshow_collection` in ipython notebook. Here is the code
```
%matplotlib
from skimage import io
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
io.use_plugin('matplotlib')
a1 = io.imread_collection('path_to_img_folder/*')
plt.figure()
io.imshow_collection(a1)
```

```
%matplotlib
from skimage import io
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
io.use_plugin('matplotlib')
a1 = io.imread('path_to_img_folder/img_file')
plt.figure()

```
Where am I going wrong?

issue_title: Handle Hawk signature with BATCH subrequests
issue_body:
Currently, BATCH operations need each request to specify their authentication. This is okay for BasicAuth but can be trickier for other authentication schemes. For instance, hawk needs each request to be signed with the content of the request, which adds complexity on the client side for almost no benefit.

One way to solve this and make it easier for clients would be to allow the specification of credentials in the BATCH request headers, and do not need to specify it during the sub-requests.

issue_title: Add optional UUID based base models
issue_body:
All the OpenWISP 2 models have uuid based IDs except django-x509.

We need to have uuid based fields for django-x509 too, but since this app is also used by projects that are not related to openwisp, we could keep this feature optional.

Tasks:

- [ ] create an abstract `BaseUUID` model which inherits BaseX509 which has a uuid field exactly as https://github.com/openwisp/openwisp-utils/blob/master/openwisp_utils/base.py#L13
- [ ] create two additional abstract models: `AbstractUUIDCa` and `AbstractUUIDCert`, which inherit `BaseUUID`
- [ ] document how to use these models in the section [Extending django-x509](https://github.com/openwisp/django-x509#extending-django-x509)

issue_title: OSX + python3: Subprocess and str encoding issues
issue_body:
I tried packaging a simple app using buildozer, without immediate success:
https://gist.github.com/johanneshk/a67231c476c18ed93cfb
First the suprocess call (osx.py, line 155) made problems, since it calls the wrong python executable. On my system python is version 2.7, so the subprocess should call python3 rather than python (or even better the python version shipped within Kivy.app?). I hacked this by replacing python with python3 in line 155 of osx.py. 

Then I encountered another error:
```
  File "package_app.py", line 95, in fill_meta
    rootObject['NSHumanReadableCopyright'] = arguments.get('--author').decode('utf-8')
AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'decode'
```
Here I simply removed the decode part. After these two hacks the build process worked without problems.

So I guess

* the appropriate python executable has to be called
* make the decode part python 3 proof?

Unfortunately I don't feel confident to fix this myself. 

I hope this makes sense and thanks for your work!

issue_title: Limit member exposition from an instance
issue_body:
Hi,

firstly I would like to thank you guys for this great library.

I would like to know if there is a way for limiting the members I can return to Fire in my functions. Let me give you an example about how I (half) resolved this:

```
import fire

class Obj:
    def __init__(self):
        self.safe = 'safe!'

    def dangerous(self):
        print('dangerous!')

class App:
    def show(self):
        obj = Obj()
        return {'safe': obj.safe}

if __name__ == '__main__':
    fire.Fire(App, name='app')
```

This makes the job as I expect, although the `--help` functionality is not as friendly as I would need since, without any parameter, `show` returns the dictionary (as one could expect):

```
$ python app.py show 
safe: safe!
```
But, instead, I would need to have a behaviour similar to the following:

```
$ python app.py show --help
Usage:       app show 
             app show safe
```

Is it possible to enforce to show the help output if no parameter is provided to `show` instead of evaluating all it? Maybe using a dictionary for restricting the access to some members is not the right way to do it. I have also tried to return a `fire.Fire` instance but then I lose the execution chain in the usage (i.e. `app show ...`).

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: [need advice] Do not cache development versions
issue_body:
Hi!

In our team we use pip-accel to accelerate virtualenv creation on Teamcity buildfarm. We install a lot of packages, including some packages from internal repository.

E.g.: build script executes `pip-accel -i http://my-index-url -r requirements.txt`, where requirements.txt is taken from git repository and contains `my-package==1.0.0`.  But if I want to develop new version (1.0.1) of `my-package`, I change a line requirements.txt to be like `git+https://my-package-repo.git@my-development-branch`.

At this moment pip-accel caches my development code on build agent as 1.0.1 or 1.0.0 version (whenever I've changed version in setup.py in my branch or not). Next build on this agent may have correct `my-package==1.0.0`, but it will load incorrect package version from pip-accel cache.

The only idea is to set version in setup.py to be like `0.0.0.development` to be sure that pip-accel will not broke any other caches. But it's uncomfortable to do it by hands every time; also you need to do it with first commit to development branch and remember to replace it with correct version after all development but before merging in to master branch.

The swagger spec states that operationId is optional.  We should auto-generate one if it does not exist--there is a simple algorithm that can be used here:

https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-codegen/blob/master/modules/swagger-codegen/src/main/java/io/swagger/codegen/DefaultCodegen.java#L1086-L1107

I was testing another python library in IronPython which calls munch, I found  some errors when importing munch in IronPython. Here's what I did:

```
>>>import munch
Runtime error (ArgumentTypeException): unicode_escape_decode() takes no arguments (1 given)
Traceback:
  line 337, in <module>, "C:\Anaconda2\Lib\site-packages\munch\__init__.py"
  line 6, in script
  line 14, in u, "C:\Anaconda2\Lib\site-packages\munch\python3_compat.py"
```

I had consulted @dglassman for this issue, and here is his solution:
The conflicts are in python3_compat.py. "The issue is that munch includes a shim for compatibility with Python3, but IronPython doesn't understand it. And since we're working with Python 2.7, we don't need it." 

The line 14 of the original python3_compat.py was:
`return codecs.unicode_escape_decode(string)[0]`

Is replaced with:
`return unicode(string)`

After I made this change, the munch library is imported successfully. 

This might be a potential issue for IronPython users, if the solution is correct, please make the updates in next release.

issue_title: Remove support for class based decorator
issue_body:
Capturing offline discussions for a public discussion.

Currently a decorator can be written as a `function` (accepting a single positional argument `props`) or a `class` (with a single `render` method accepting a single positional argument `props`) which has a few issues:
- Nothing is passed to the `__init__` of the `class` but instead everything is passed to `props` which makes the `class` quite useless. Moving the props to the `__init__` will either create  boilerplate or will force the user to inherit from a custom class which does that for them. In short, it's not clear which benefit there is to use a class.
- To keep naming consistent, functions are names are camel cased (so when you update your config file, you don't have to think whether it's a function or a class) which gets the linter to complain. The library should not encourage non-PEP8 compliant code.

We were therefore thinking of removing support for class based decorators. Any thoughts?

**Plugin System**
- [ ] **Register/Unregister mechanism**: find a way for user to easily find and install new plugin. Probably like Sublime Text's Package Control. It a JSON file stores on github, user refresh periodicaly and are updated of new available packages on merge. Probably on https://github.come/Guake/plugins/plugins.xml
- [ ] **Python Event system** : Plugin will be able to react in python to some event:
- [ ] **2 Person review** : for plugin to be available to user, at least 2 person need code review
- [ ] **onion deployment**: plugins to be deployed using canary-like deployment. Users are packed into n different layers ("stable/unstable/cutting-edge" for instance) so they test plugins in advance

issue_title: When not managing IAM role, don't require permission to pass role
issue_body:
We use a continuous deployment role with limited privileges. In particular, the deployment role has read-only IAM permissions. When first deploying the function, we use a privileged account that sets up the Lambda IAM role and policy. (We turn off policy autogeneration.)

Up to and including Chalice 0.9, the deploy command correctly did not try to write anything to IAM. However, starting with Chalice 0.10, the call to update_function_configuration unconditionally tries to update the Lambda IAM role ARN, even if it has not changed. This results in an error of this form:

```
 An error occurred (AccessDeniedException) when calling the 
 UpdateFunctionConfiguration operation: User: 
 arn:aws:iam::ACCT:user/CI-USER is not authorized to perform: 
 iam:PassRole on resource: arn:aws:iam::ACCT:role/lambda-dev
```
Obviously, allowing iam:PassRole would defeat the whole purpose/premise of restricting privileges of the CI/CD account.

I propose that the update_function_configuration step compute a diff of what has actually changed, and not try to modify Lambda settings that have not changed.

issue_title: cuDNN cannot be found by CMake
issue_body:
I noticed CMake was consistently failing to find cuDNN for me, even though all other CUDA libraries and include files (which are located in the same places) are found.

From a quick skim of `CMakeLists.txt`, it seems that on [line 155](https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/91431dc16e2844e090f45b4ddb367dd9286b9964/CMakeLists.txt#L155) it is checking whether the `CUDNN_ROOT` variable is set, and if it is it proceeds to call the `find_cudnn()` function.  I don't really understand the `CMakeLists.txt` format, but if I am reading it correctly then `CUDNN_ROOT` is only ever set inside `find_cudnn()`, so the `if` on line 155 has no chance of succeeding.

Setting `CUDNN_ROOT` manually solves the problem for me.

issue_title: Size/length limits for Datastore properties are enforced differently than NDB
issue_body:
The Datastore/NDB documentation states that `BlobProperty` (and other `blob` property types) can be of unlimited length (given that an Entity can still only be 1MB).
https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/python/ndb/properties#types

When using an unindexed `JsonProperty` (which is a `blob` type), the gcloud API enforces a 1500 byte limit, which creates a discrepancy between the GAE Datastore API, documentation, and our expectations.

Here's a stacktrace snippet:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/script/datastore_helpers/gcloud_populate_datastore.py", line 266, in <module>
    populate_with_contents_of_file(path, args.profile)
  File "/script/datastore_helpers/gcloud_populate_datastore.py", line 229, in populate_with_contents_of_file
    api.put(gae_entities)
  File "/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gcloud/datastore/api.py", line 272, in put
    current.commit()
  File "/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gcloud/datastore/batch.py", line 207, in commit
    response = self.connection.commit(self._dataset_id, self.mutation)
  File "/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gcloud/datastore/connection.py", line 324, in commit
    datastore_pb.CommitResponse)
  File "/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gcloud/datastore/connection.py", line 108, in _rpc
    data=request_pb.SerializeToString())
  File "/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gcloud/datastore/connection.py", line 85, in _request
    raise make_exception(headers, content, use_json=False)
gcloud.exceptions.BadRequest: 400 The value of property "variations.actions" is longer than 1500 bytes.
```

FWIW: We're still using version 0.4.3 of this gcloud library, but since this response comes from your API, upgrading would not appear to resolve it.

Is there a way to work around this? Should we expect the Cloud Datastore API to have the same limits as the GAE Datastore/NDB API?

issue_title: pex cannot resolve local dependencies with dash in the name
issue_body:
pex does not work well with a local pypi. So I followed the work around described here https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/270 . However, if you have dependencies with a dash in the name like javaobj-py3 or asn1-modules, pex fails. The following demonstrates the issue.

First download the wheel:
```
pip wheels -w wheels javaobj-py3==0.2.3
ERROR: unknown command "wheels" - maybe you meant "wheel"
[root@4d3e53cc5bc5 newdir]# pip wheel -w wheels javaobj-py3==0.2.3
Collecting javaobj-py3==0.2.3
  Downloading https://my-local-pypi/pypi/simple/javaobj-py3/javaobj-py3-0.2.3-py2.py3-none-any.whl
  Saved ./wheels/javaobj-py3-0.2.3-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Skipping javaobj-py3, due to already being wheel.
```

Now try creating a pex file with it.
```
pex --no-index --repo=wheels -o this_wont_work.pex javaobj-py3==0.2.3
Could not satisfy all requirements for javaobj-py3==0.2.3:
    javaobj-py3==0.2.3
```

Here is a workaround which will create a hard link to all python modules and replace the a dash with underscore.

```
wheel_dir=./wheels && for old in $(ls ${wheel_dir}); do new=$(echo $old | sed -e 's/-\([a-z]\)/_\1/'); if [[ $new != $old ]]; then ln "${wheel_dir}/${old}" "${wheel_dir}/${new}"; fi; done
pex --no-index --repo=wheels -o this_workaround_works.pex javaobj-py3==0.2.3
[root@4d3e53cc5bc5 newdir]# pex --no-index --repo=wheels -o this_workaround_works.pex javaobj-py3==0.2.3
[root@4d3e53cc5bc5 newdir]# ls -la
total 464
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root     62 May  1 23:42 .
drwxrwxrwt. 1 root root     26 May  1 23:42 ..
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 471703 May  1 23:42 this_workaround_works.pex
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    152 May  1 23:26 wheels
```

issue_title: Global flag that dumps the fingerprint payload for any target/task combo
issue_body:
Debugging cache misses is still a dark art at times. A good example might be a CI run that operates over a target and caches the result, but running that SHA locally reports a miss.

When I have to dig into those situations, I generally do so manually - adding breakpoints in the
various environments and comparing contents of the payload. But that gets very hard to reason about and the debugging process has not scaled well to my teammates.

One useful idea I have considered to make this easier to debug is a global flag, something along the lines of

`./pants --payload compile src/to:target`

This could dump a json file with the target and the payload as consumed by the requested execute block. This could be a console task perhaps, as opposed to logging.

I am posting an issue in the hopes that I find some time to take a look at doing this someday.

As mentioned in `context.py`, we need to port computation of the file count metric to using the rust `Graph` API:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/goal/context.py#L181-L186

This will help to unblock #5639 by providing an implementation of this useful metric that does not require any `Target` objects to be created.

----

Doing this will involve a few steps.
1. Add an API to `src/rust/engine/src/graph.rs` that given a set of `NodeKey` roots, returns the count of `DigestFile` Nodes below those roots. This will involve using `Graph::walk` ([example](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/b3f7124c9076be3bf4e1cb369bfeda16f3b963f6/src/rust/engine/src/graph.rs#L218-L221)) and some of the logic from [Graph::all_digests](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/b3f7124c9076be3bf4e1cb369bfeda16f3b963f6/src/rust/engine/src/graph.rs#L456-L472) in a new method that walks and counts digests it encounters.
2. Expose the new `Graph` API from (1) in `src/rust/engine/src/lib.rs`. The signature there would probably be something like:
    ```
    #[no_mangle]
    pub extern "C" fn graph_count_file_digests(
      scheduler_ptr: *mut Scheduler,
      execution_request_ptr: *mut ExecutionRequest,
    ) {
    ```
3. Update [src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py#L302) to record all root `Nodes` that have been requested via [LocalScheduler::execution_request](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py#L378-L399) since the `Scheduler` was created. issue_title: Invoke cli.update() every time plugins are updated or changed
issue_body:
## Summary

As per #1615, there is an issue about .pex files with identical version files. Since we haven't pin-pointed the source of the issue, we can only guarantee consistency in migrations and static assets by running `cli.update()` every time kolibri starts.

So this is not an issue, until we revert this behaviour in #1498 

I think the necessary change would be to make the version file contain BOTH kolibri version and a list of everything in INSTALLED_APPS, moreover adding their version numbers. I'm not sure if loading `application.__init__` is safe for all apps, but since `django.setup()` is already called, I would assume it is.

## System information

Future kolibri :)

## How to reproduce

1. Install Kolibri + plugins
1. Upgrade a plugin or change plugin configuration

issue_title: When pulling docker images without a tag, we get ALL tags
issue_body:
This was introduced as an issue in PR #96.

This happens both with implicit pulls and explicit pulls, including running BusyBox.

Specifically, there is no equivalent of *not* specifying `--all-tags` for the pull command when accessed through python.  For BusyBox, we can fix this by adding `:latest` to the image name.  For the general case, before we pull or run an image, we would need to check if there is a tag or digest.  If not, we would need to query if there is already a local image of the specified name, and, if not, ask the remote server (probably hub.docker.com) what tags are available, and then use one of them (`latest` being preferred).

issue_title: allow exabgp to start without any neighbours
issue_body:
It would simplify our orchestration if we could start a fixed amount of instances on a host without any neighbours configured.
Over time neighbours would be added and exabgp instructed to reload its configuration.
Currently exabgp just exits.
Thanks,
Florian

Currently, ExaBGP 4 is supporting python 3 and python 2 (even though it should not be relied upon).

> ExaBGP 4.0 and later are targeting python3 when ExaBGP 3.4 and previous versions are python2 applications. At the moment, it is possible to run ExaBGP 4.0 using python2.7 but this backward compatibility should not be relied upon.

While in many cases supporting both version can be straightforward, I believe that for ExaBGP case, supporting py3 only would make things easier has:
* you wont be forced in supporting slight language differences
* bytes vs strings will be much more straightforward
* the event loop could move to a natively supported framework (asyncio) vs building your own
* IP parsing could benefit from ipaddress module which ships by default with py3
* testing also becomes easier

Overall, python 3 is widely deployed in most distros. Python 3.5 supports the new syntactic sugar that makes asyncio easier, adds type hints support.

I appreciate that python 2.7 could be supported, but I think the project would benefit from only supporting 1 python version for simplicity.

Replaces https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/issues/381

### What is wrong?

The way things are currently structured there is a class which combines the logic of the `Chain` with the necessary networking logic for syncing with the ethereum network.

This duel purpose class is problematic for the following reasons.

- *tight* coupling between the `Chain` abstraction and whatever networking abstraction we are using. 
- error prone API where networking code may interact with chain APIs which are both blocking and processor heavy, causing networking timeouts and other networking problems.
- makes decoupling the JSON-RPC server process from the networking process difficult.

### How can it be fixed

Lets split the networking specific code into a new class, `NetworkClient`.  We can use a naming convention like `LightClient` and `FullClient` for various different syncing approaches, but the `Client` suffix feels appropriate.

The `NetworkClient` would be given a `Chain` object and *probably* the low level database connection or maybe *both* the low level database and the high level `ChainDB`.  Interactions with the `chain` or chain databases can be done using `Executors` from asyncio.

To facilitate the JSON-RPC server we can also create a `ChainOverNetwork` class which implements the `Chain` API.  This class would be given access to the `NetworkClient` instance and would implement the necessary logic to retrieve chain data like Blocks or Receipts over the network.

issue_title: p2p.Server must ensure PeerPool is always running
issue_body: Now that [PeerPool no longer restarts itself](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/678), we need to make sure`p2p.Server` periodically check if it's still running, and possibly restart it if not. This is needed because p2p.Server runs the PeerPool in the background (with `asyncio.ensure_future()`)

issue_title: Uploading environment to Anaconda cloud, fails with 400
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm following the instructions here[1].  These are the two commands that docs say can be used to upload the environment.

>conda env export -n my-environment
>conda env upload my-environment

The second command fails with:
>CondaEnvRuntimeError: Runtime error: Unable to locate environment file: environment.yml

It seems that it's expecting the first command to write the output to environment.yml, however it just writes it to stdout. This is easily fixed by redirecting stdout to environment.yml.

Once that is done, and the upload command is run again. I get a 400 response. Investigating further, the specific error is is this:
>requirements, announce and description are required

The root of the problem seems to be the following. `conda_env.utils.Uploader.upload()` first checks that the packagename already exists (via `is_ready()`). If it doesn't exist, it calls `self.binstar.add_release(self.username, self.packagename, self.version, {}, '', '')`

Those last three arguments of that method are named `requirements`, `description`, and `release_attrs`. I replaced that call with the following:

`self.binstar.add_release(self.username, self.packagename, self.version, {'python': '2.7'}, 'blah', {'description': 'foobar'})`

I just guessed what actual params to use here. Anyway, that failed with a 404 (I didn't step through and investigate), but a subsequent call did work, and my environment was uploaded successfully.

I'm not too aufait with Anaconda Cloud, but it seems like everything is a 'package' (even environments), and the server needs those fields to be populated.

These are the versions of anaconda-client and conda:  
>anaconda-client           1.6.3
>conda                     4.3.16

I suspect it's either PEBKAC or I'm running old versions of anaconda-client (even though conda tells me it is up to date), otherwise more people would be having this problem I suspect.

Cheers

issue_title: Navigator: The following conda create command should work?
issue_body:
**On conda 4.0.2**

On navigator we explicitly pass the channels to conda commands so when creating a new env with both python and R, the command that runs is

```bash
conda create --yes --quiet --json --mkdir --name rpy35 python=3.5 jupyter r-essentials --override-channels --channel https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro --channel https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free --channel https://conda.anaconda.org/r
```

This results in **Using Anaconda Cloud api site https://api.anaconda.org** and it just hangs...

I don't fully understand why that command hangs while this one, (assuming no condarc exists)

```bash
conda create --name rpy35 python=3.5 jupyter r-essentials --channel https://conda.anaconda.org/r
```

Works...

They should be in theory the exact same thing under the hood, I am missing something @kalefranz?

Hi!
During installing new Anaconda I have 
![installanacondaproblemprompt2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19995321/44310850-c7386400-a3e5-11e8-90bb-23774e400530.png)
After I use "continue" after this error, I dont have conda.exe, navigator in main menu is epcent etc.

I can install the latest Anaconda only 4.2.0 with python 3.5.
My assumption is that problem with qt-5.6.2 (installation)

I am trying to instal more than 10 times.

I read about versioning quite in detail in [PEP 440](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440) and conda's effort to include it #1601...

That seems to work very well, but I was wondering, whether one could exclude pre-releases from conda? 

If I have two packages: 
```
1.0.0
1.0.1dev1
```
... then clearly the second is newer than the first. So if I call `conda install mypkg` I'll get the second. 

Is there a way, so that conda ignores developement releases, if not specifically demanded?  [PEP 440 - Handling of Pre-Releases](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#handling-of-pre-releases) says:

> Dependency resolution tools SHOULD also allow users to request the following alternative behaviours:
> - accepting pre-releases for all version specifiers
> - excluding pre-releases for all version specifiers (reporting an error or warning if a pre-release is already installed locally, or if a pre-release is the only way to satisfy a particular specifier)

Conda currently does the first option by default, can I achieve the second?

issue_title: Time complexity of AlignmentFile class methods references and lengths
issue_body:
Recently I noticed very slow parsing of a BAM file with 500k references, which I thought pretty mysterious. 

The loop iterated over, in part, one of either AlignmentFile.references` and `AlignmentFile.lengths`. The other was being dereferenced inside the loop.

Eg.
```
for n, reflen in enumerate(bam.lengths):
    refname = bam.references[n]
   ...
```
As these appear as arrays in pysam documentation, I thought this would be O(1) but it clearly was not. Inspecting , libcalignmentfile.pyx I guess the answer is that tuples are built _every call_!

Could the documentation be amended or better yet can memoization be applied to the Cython methods?

issue_title: Add support for raw ARM API requests
issue_body:
Any plans to add a raw request mode to **az**?

Something like:
```
$ az raw get https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/redis/providers/Microsoft.Cache/Redis/{RedisCacheName}?api-version=2016-04-01
```

Basically what [ARMClient ](https://github.com/projectkudu/armclient) is doing. There's an opportunity to merge functionality into a single CLI tool.

Sometimes, there are commands you use over and over again that can be long to type out and not straightforward to remember.

The 'alias' component would contain mappings between user-supplied commands and user-supplied aliases.

```
$ az alias create --command "az vm list --query '[].{name:name,os:storageProfile.osDisk.osType}'" --name vm-os-types
```

```
$ az alias list
[
    {
        "command": "az vm list --query '[].{name:name,os:storageProfile.osDisk.osType}'",
        "alias": "vm-os-types"
    },
        ...
]
```

```
$ az alias show --name vm-os-types
{
    "command": "az vm list --query '[].{name:name,os:storageProfile.osDisk.osType}'",
    "alias": "vm-os-types"
}
```

```
$ az alias update --name vm-os-types --command "az vm list --query '[].{name:name,osType:storageProfile.osDisk.osType}'"
```

```
$ az alias delete --name vm-os-types
```

**Implementation Details:**
- The config of aliases could be stored in `~/.azure/alias.config` using the [config format](https://docs.python.org/2/library/configparser.html) used elsewhere in the CLI.
- When the 'alias' component is loaded, it can dynamically add the aliased commands to the command table.
- So when an alias is called, the 'alias' component can run the full az command under-the-hood (should output the full command executed to logger.warning).
- *Could possibly extend this to include parameters in aliases also.*

**Considerations:**
- If the command to be aliased is entered on the command line, how can quotes be escaped properly? Ideally, the user wouldn't have to deal with this.

issue_title: Add support to enable HTTPS only for webapps
issue_body:
Reference
https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/appserviceteam/2017/12/12/november-2017-app-service-update/

If a user has the Debian version of `requests`, they _may_ run into the following error.

```
  File "/home/ls/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrest/__init__.py", line 27, in <module>
    from .configuration import Configuration
  File "/home/ls/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrest/configuration.py", line 39, in <module>
    from .pipeline import (
  File "/home/ls/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrest/pipeline.py", line 36, in <module>
    from requests.packages.urllib3 import Retry
ImportError: No module named packages.urllib3
```

Observations:
- The version of [requests available on Debian](https://packages.debian.org/sid/python-requests) does not have the requests.packages sub-package.
- The file list for Debian python-requests does not include requests.packages.urllib3 - https://packages.debian.org/sid/all/python-requests/filelist

- In Debian, urllib3 is shipped separately as you can see in the dependencies through the link above.
- Here's the file list for urllib3 - https://packages.debian.org/sid/all/python-urllib3/filelist

Not 100% sure of the following but I suspect that this may be a cause.
Even if I do have the pip version of `requests` (which includes `requests.packages....), I may still run into this error if I also have the Debian package installed.
Based on how Python loads modules from PATH, the Debian package in /dist-packages might get loaded instead of the pip package in /site-packages.

```
$ python
Python 2.7.6 (default, Jun 22 2015, 17:58:13)

Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.

/home/debekoe/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages
/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages
```

Is the solution to try the import `requests.packages.urllib3`, if it fails, fall back to `urllib3....`?

Reference links:
